title: podman machine init fails on Intel Macbook Pro with Error: downloading VM
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Podman machine init fails.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. brew install podman

2. podman init machine

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Error: downloading VM image https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/next-devel/builds/36.20220511.dev.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: 404 Not Found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Expected to init a new virtual machine

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I checked the Fedora url, and it is not reachable. Seems the builds are completely removed.

**Output of `podman --version`:**

```
podman version 4.1.0
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/_ping": dial unix ///var/folders/gs/zhlk6h152dv48vd4yy5nlvw00000gn/T/podman-run--1/podman/podman.sock: connect: no such file or directory
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
same output ad podman version.
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman: stable 4.1.0 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.1.0 (174 files, 47.7MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-05-12 at 02:22:52
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

```
Yes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

```
MacOS Montery version 12.3.1
```

**Workarround for development or testing environment**

Add parameter --image-path
```
podman machine init --image-path=https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/builds/36.20220511.dev.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz

Downloading VM image: fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: done  
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:

        podman machine start
```

comments.body:
For some reason podman 4.1.0 ignores image-path keyword:

```
> podman machine init --image-path=stable
Error: downloading VM image https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/next-devel/builds/36.20220511.dev.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz: 404 Not Found
```

As a workaround something can be used
```
$ curl -L -O https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/prod/streams/next/builds/36.20220507.1.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-36.20220507.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz 
$ podman machine init --image-path=fedora-coreos-36.20220507.1.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz 
```

Worked around by going to the [CoreOS download page](https://getfedora.org/en/coreos/download?tab=metal_virtualized&stream=testing&arch=aarch64), finding the URL for the QEMU image in my architecture, and passing that to `podman machine init`.

e.g. for M1 Macs:

```sh
podman machine init --image-path=https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/prod/streams/testing/builds/36.20220505.2.0/aarch64/fedora-coreos-36.20220505.2.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz
```

The correct URLs that are under the `fedorapeople` FQDN would be the following:

M1 macOS: https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/builds/36.20220511.dev.0/aarch64/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz

Intel macOS: https://fedorapeople.org/groups/podman/testing/builds/36.20220511.dev.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-36.20220511.dev.0-qemu.x86_64.qcow2.xz

**Note**: The difference is lack of `/next/`
